topic: Tidal N2 (larger lunar elliptic semi-diurnal) period
change_summary: The c2 (2026-04-08) major-rewrite recommendations were integrated: detail_evidence now leads with the eccentricity argument (N2 amplitude ratio N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 encodes lunar eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549); finding differentiates N2 from M2/S2 by citing the eccentricity requirement explicitly; detail_claim expanded from 33 to 15 chars (actually shortened to "Tidal N2 period" — table-compact) with context moved to detail_claim-proper; detail_verdict_text expanded ~10× (127 → 1,315 chars) to add the post-hoc/trivial-transcription structural argument and the amplitude-ratio eccentricity test. Net: WIN-051 is now the strongest tidal WIN in narrative terms, and the verdict holds.
our_argument_summary: N2 (12.6583 h) is uniquely eccentricity-dependent among the five tidal WINs: its very existence in standard tidal theory arises from the lunar perigee/apogee distance variation, and its amplitude ratio to M2 directly measures the moon's orbital eccentricity (N2/M2 ≈ 0.19; observed lunar e ≈ 0.0549). The dome model has never specified an orbital eccentricity for its local moon at 2,534 km altitude, never derived one from dome geometry, and never calculated N2 amplitude or the N2/M2 ratio from dome parameters. Monitor.py has no N2 domain — this is a static text claim with no computational support. The dome's own OPEN-007 concedes that lunar orbital mechanics have no working dome-native explanation, creating an explicit self-contradiction: N2 is declared a confirmed prediction while the orbital mechanics generating it are admitted to be unresolved. Verdict: Self-Contradicted. The review also stacks a Part 3.1b structural failure (trivial-transcription / post-hoc) on top of the scientific critique.

tldr_review.notes: Both TLDRs faithfully compress the expanded content. tldr_evidence identifies N2 as "the most revealing tidal WIN" and cites the N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 amplitude ratio — consistent with the expanded detail_evidence. tldr_verdict says the ratio is "impossible on the dome's gear-driven circular orbit" — punchy, and consistent with the detail. Importantly, the TLDRs do NOT repeat the "3e" numerical error in the expanded prose (see holes_found #1) — they stay at the qualitative level ("directly encodes"). So the primary numeric issue doesn't propagate to the reader-visible TLDR layer. Minor: the TLDR uses "12.658 hours" (four significant figures) while detail_evidence uses "12.6580" (five). Harmless imprecision.

relevant_files: raw-text/08-live-power.txt (monitor.py domain listing — M2, K1, S2 present; N2 and O1 absent)
raw-text/02-wins.txt (no N2 mention in wins page content extracted from the dome site)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No computational pipeline for N2 exists on the dome side. Monitor.py tracks only M2 (Lomb-Scargle amplitude extraction at 12.42 h period, tolerance 0.1%), K1 (23.9345 h, tolerance 0.01%), and S2 (12.0 h, tolerance 0.01%). N2 has no domain entry, no data fetch, no threshold, and no comparison logic. The "confirmation" for N2 is a static wins.json assertion only.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — there is no monitor.py entry for N2 at all. It is a pure static-text claim in wins.json with no validation code anywhere in the dome repository. Our review correctly identifies this as monitoring:none.
data_sources_used: N2 period = 12.6583 h (standard harmonic constituent value, tabulated since Doodson 1921 and present in every modern tidal constituent database including NOAA CO-OPS and IHO)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Dome defender options: (a) claim tidal forcing is aetheric not gravitational, so Keplerian eccentricity formulas don't apply — but then the defender must derive the 0.19 ratio from some other parameter; (b) claim N2 is "period match only, amplitude is separate" — but this concedes the prediction is trivial; (c) claim the local moon's gear-driven trajectory has non-uniform angular velocity that mimics orbital eccentricity — this is the strongest defense (see advocate_mode) and requires the dome to publish a derivation, which they have not done.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: One moderate wording issue: detail_verdict_text contains "the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable." For N2 specifically there IS no live fetch call in monitor.py. The language appears templated from WIN-045 (M2) / WIN-049 (K1) where a live fetch exists. For N2 the situation is strictly worse — there's no fetch at all — but the current phrasing accidentally implies a fetch exists. See holes_found #3.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: raw-text/08-live-power.txt enumerates monitor.py domains: Schumann, Tesla, NMP Longitude, M2 Tidal, K1 Tidal, Equatorial Gravity, κ, SAA Decay, Polaris Excess, Aetheric Redshift Scale, S2 Solar Tidal, P-wave Shadow, CMB Axis, Solar Angular Diameter, NMP Drift. No N2 domain. No Lomb-Scargle extraction for N2 frequency. No N2 tolerance, no fetch, no comparison.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: N2 (12.6583 h) is a standard tidal harmonic constituent catalogued since Doodson (1921). Standard tidal theory explains N2 as the amplitude modulation of M2 by the moon's varying distance at perigee vs. apogee, with N2/M2 ≈ (7/2)·e ≈ 0.19 from the leading-order eccentricity expansion of the tide-generating potential. The dome adopts the period and relabels the mechanism as "aetheric" without changing any numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Doodson (1921) derived the complete harmonic expansion including N2 over a century ago. N2 appears in every textbook and every modern tidal constituent database. The dome model added N2 to wins.json in V51.0 (April 2026). This is a century-old tabulated value adopted and declared confirmed — the archetypal post-hoc prediction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No dome-specific derivation exists. N2's physical origin requires orbital eccentricity — a parameter the dome's local moon has never been assigned. The dome's OPEN-007 concedes lunar orbital mechanics are unresolved. There is no formula in monitor.py, formulas.json, or the wins-page content that starts from dome geometry and arrives at 12.6583 h, let alone the N2/M2 amplitude ratio.

holes_found.description: Numerical precision error in detail_evidence: the text states "N2/M2 ≈ 3e for small eccentricities, yielding e ≈ 0.063 (close to the actual e ≈ 0.0549)." The leading-order coefficient is (7/2)e, not 3e. Using 3e with observed N2/M2 = 0.19 back-solves to e = 0.0633, which differs from the known lunar eccentricity 0.0549 by 15.4%. Calling that "close" undersells a much stronger argument. The correct factor (7/2)e gives e = 0.0543 from N2/M2 = 0.19 — within 1% of actual. Derivation: in the Darwin-Doodson expansion of the tide-generating potential for a Keplerian orbit, the N2 equilibrium coefficient is 0.17387 and M2 is 0.90812 (Doodson 1921, Table II); ratio 0.17387/0.90812 = 0.1915, and the leading-order eccentricity sideband amplitude from the expansion of (a/r)^3 is (7/2)·e, giving (7/2)·0.0549 = 0.1922. This makes the argument stronger, not weaker.
holes_found.recommendation: Change "N2/M2 ≈ 3e for small eccentricities, yielding e ≈ 0.063 (close to the actual e ≈ 0.0549)" to "N2/M2 ≈ (7/2)·e at leading order (from the Darwin-Doodson expansion of the tide-generating potential for a Keplerian orbit; Doodson 1921, Table II); the observed N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 back-solves to e ≈ 0.054, matching the measured lunar eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549 to better than 1%." This sharpens the quantitative test: the dome must not merely produce SOME eccentricity, but one that threads the needle at 0.054 via a first-principles derivation it has never provided.

holes_found.description: Advocate-mode vulnerability: the detail_evidence does not preempt the "non-uniform gear motion" defense. A thoughtful dome defender can argue the local moon's gear-driven trajectory has non-uniform angular velocity (a "dome eccentricity" proxy) that could in principle mimic the N2/M2 amplitude ratio without invoking Keplerian eccentricity. Our current text treats orbital eccentricity as the only possible origin of N2, leaving this rhetorical opening unpatched.
holes_found.recommendation: Add a parenthetical after the amplitude-ratio sentence: "A dome defender might invoke non-uniform angular velocity of the gear-driven moon as a proxy for eccentricity — but this merely relocates the problem: the dome would then need to derive the specific gear-speed profile that produces N2/M2 = 0.19 at lunar-month timescales from its geometry, and publish the derivation. No such calculation exists in the dome repository as of V51.1."

holes_found.description: Verdict-text code claim is templated and inaccurate for N2 specifically. detail_verdict_text states: "Under the dome author's own definition ('prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled'), the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable." For N2, there is NO live fetch call in monitor.py (monitoring:none). The phrasing appears imported from M2/K1/S2 where a live fetch does exist. The misapplication is a gift to a dome defender who can fact-check the code and say "you're describing a live fetch that doesn't exist; your review doesn't understand our code."
holes_found.recommendation: Revise that sentence for N2 to: "For N2 specifically, the situation is strictly worse: there is no monitor.py automation at all — no predicted value, no fetch, no comparison. The claim lives only as static text on the wins page, so the dome author's 'timestamped before data' test cannot be applied to N2 at all."

holes_found.description: Period precision: detail_evidence states "N2 (12.6580 hours)" but the standard value is 12.6583 h (Doodson 1921 harmonic period). This is a 3×10⁻⁴ h ≈ 1 second discrepancy — functionally irrelevant but cosmetically a defender could point to "your review can't even copy our period correctly." tldr_evidence uses "12.658 hours" which rounds acceptably; the detail should match.
holes_found.recommendation: Change "12.6580" to "12.6583" in detail_evidence. Negligible semantic effect; removes a trivial fact-check opportunity.

holes_found.description: Moon-setting segue is rhetorically jarring. The paragraph beginning "The dome model's published literature does not address the moon's disappearance mechanism — a basic kinematic requirement…" does argue correctly that unresolved elementary kinematics implies unresolved deeper orbital mechanics, but for a reader focused on N2's amplitude ratio, this feels like a digression. A sharper construction would connect it back to N2: "if the dome has not yet solved why the moon appears to set, it has not reached the deeper orbital mechanics (eccentricity, nodal regression, apsidal precession) that N2 requires." The current text does say this; it just reads as a separate paragraph rather than a structural contrast.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional: tighten the transition. "Before we even reach the orbital-mechanics regime that generates N2, the dome's published literature does not address the moon's disappearance mechanism — a basic kinematic requirement on a flat disc with a locally orbiting moon. A model that has not resolved elementary kinematics a fortiori has not resolved apsidal precession or eccentricity modulation." Non-blocking.

stronger_arguments.description: The N2/M2 ratio is a ONE-PARAMETER falsification test. Standard tidal theory predicts N2/M2 = (7/2)·e + O(e³) from a single astronomical constant: the moon's orbital eccentricity e ≈ 0.0549. Measured N2/M2 ≈ 0.19 matches this single-parameter prediction to within ~1%. Any dome-native mechanism for N2 must produce the SAME RATIO from whatever dome parameters it invokes, and must do so without a tuning knob — otherwise the "prediction" is retrodictive fitting. Framing N2 this way converts it from "we don't know where the dome would get it" (current language) to "the dome must produce 0.19 from first principles using parameters it has published — a specific numerical target." This is a more aggressive and more rigorous stance.
sources: Doodson, A.T. (1921). The Harmonic Development of the Tide-Generating Potential. Proc. Royal Society A, 100(704), 305–329. doi:10.1098/rspa.1921.0088
Pugh, D.T. & Woodworth, P.L. (2014). Sea-Level Science. Cambridge University Press, §3.2 on harmonic constituent relationships

stronger_arguments.description: Nodal modulation of N2 (18.613-year cycle). Beyond the 12.66 h period and the 0.19 amplitude ratio, N2 exhibits a secondary amplitude modulation at the lunar nodal regression period (18.613 years). This is a direct consequence of the moon's orbital plane precession — yet another orbital parameter the dome has never specified for its local moon. If the dome claims N2, it must also reproduce this 18.613-year modulation. Historical tide records from the past century (e.g., Battery NYC, Portsmouth UK) show the nodal cycle clearly; IPCC AR6 explicitly cites the 18.613-year signal as a known tidal modulator of sea-level records. This extends the falsification test from one orbital parameter (eccentricity) to two (eccentricity + nodal inclination).
sources: Foreman, M.G.G. (1977). Manual of Harmonic Analysis and Prediction of Tides. Dept. Fisheries and Oceans, Ottawa. Section 4.3.
IPCC AR6 WGI Ch. 9 Box 9.1 — cites the 18.61-yr nodal cycle as a known modulator of tide records.

stronger_arguments.description: Missing mandatory companion harmonics. A Keplerian expansion that produces N2 at (7/2)e automatically produces companion sidebands at the same order: L2 (larger lunar eccentric, ~0.028·M2), 2N2 (variational, ~0.024·M2), and μ2 (variational, ~0.029·M2). These appear in every tide-gauge harmonic analysis. The dome claims only three of the Doodson catalog (M2, K1, S2) in monitor.py and adds two more (O1, N2) as static-text WINs. If the dome accepts N2 as aetheric-confirmed, it must also explain L2, 2N2, and μ2 — or concede that its acceptance of N2 is selective. We don't need to run this argument all the way to completion; mentioning "N2 is one of ~12 eccentricity/nodal sidebands you haven't catalogued" raises the cost of dome-native derivation from "derive one number" to "derive a family of numbers with consistent relative amplitudes."
sources: NOAA CO-OPS Harmonic Constituents List (tidesandcurrents.noaa.gov) — lists L2, 2N2, μ2 among the 37 standard constituents used in US tide prediction.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model correctly identifies N2 as a standard tidal constituent at the correct period (to one-second precision) and correctly groups it with the other four lunar/solar-forced constituents. Listing five constituents rather than only M2 and S2 shows awareness that tidal dynamics involves multiple orbital timescales — apogee/perigee, lunar declination, nodal regression — beyond a simple two-body-at-fixed-distance picture. This is more sophisticated than a minimal flat-Earth model would attempt.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Awareness is not derivation. N2 is the only tidal constituent whose amplitude ratio to the principal tide directly encodes a fundamental orbital parameter (eccentricity). Matching the tabulated period is equivalent to reciting a constant from a reference table; it is the definition of a post-hoc claim. Without deriving the N2/M2 amplitude ratio (≈ 0.19), the 18.61-year nodal modulation, and at least the companion sidebands L2 / 2N2 / μ2 from the dome's geometry, the dome has not PREDICTED N2 — it has copied the period and left the physics unresolved. The dome's own OPEN-007 concedes this when it admits lunar orbital mechanics are unresolved, which is the self-contradiction.

advocate_mode.best_defense: A dome defender, operating in good faith, might argue as follows: "The critical review treats N2 as if its ONLY possible origin is Keplerian orbital eccentricity. But in the aetheric framework, tidal forcing is not gravitational — it is a coupling between the moon and the aether as the moon traverses its gear-driven trajectory. The geared trajectory need not have constant angular velocity; in fact, the V51.0 wins page shows the moon at 2,534 km with its specific trajectory parameters. Non-uniform angular velocity around the gear produces an amplitude modulation at a sub-harmonic of the lunar synodic period, which would manifest as a frequency sideband at (2·Ω_moon) ± Δω — and Δω can be tuned to produce the observed N2 frequency at 12.6583 h. The amplitude ratio N2/M2 = 0.19 is then a constraint on the non-uniformity parameter, which the dome has simply not yet published. This is a gap in exposition, not a failure of the model. Asking us to derive an aetheric mechanism using Keplerian formulas is strawmanning. Furthermore, OPEN-007 acknowledges lunar orbital mechanics are unresolved — this is the dome being epistemically honest, not conceding self-contradiction. An open problem is not a refutation; it is an invitation to further work."
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add two preemptive sentences to detail_evidence or detail_verdict_text: (1) "A dome-native route to N2 via non-uniform gear motion is logically possible but empirically absent — the V51.1 repository contains no calculation of a gear-speed profile yielding N2/M2 = 0.19 at 12.6583 h, and until such a calculation is published the 'prediction' remains a period match without an amplitude derivation." (2) "Declaring a phenomenon a WIN while acknowledging in OPEN-007 that the mechanism generating it is unresolved is not epistemic humility — it is the textbook definition of self-contradiction. The dome cannot simultaneously claim N2 as confirmed and lunar orbital mechanics as open; one of those labels must move." These two sentences neutralize the strongest good-faith defense in one paragraph.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: No contradictions. The five tidal WINs share the same verdict (Self-Contradicted) and the same core critique (period is trivial, dome has no way to derive amplitude or reproduce the global two-bulge pattern from a local moon at 2,534 km). Each WIN correctly differentiates its own unique signature: M2 = half a lunar day (base forcing), S2 = half a solar day (solar forcing), K1 = luni-solar diurnal (requires declination geometry), O1 = lunar diurnal (tabulated in Doodson), N2 = eccentricity-encoded amplitude (unique orbital test). No WIN overstates what another understates. The monitoring tags are internally consistent with the raw-text evidence: WIN-045/046/049 are tagged "hardcoded" and they do have monitor.py domains with static expected values; WIN-050/051 are tagged "none" and they do NOT have monitor.py domains. Minor stylistic note: WIN-050 mentions Doodson (1921) tabulated "389 tidal frequencies" but WIN-051 doesn't cite that count even though N2 is also part of the same catalog; a one-sentence cross-reference would tighten the group.

claims_checked: N2 period 12.6580 h (in review) vs canonical 12.6583 h
N2/M2 ratio ≈ 0.19 from equilibrium tidal theory
Approximation N2/M2 ≈ 3e implied by review text
Approximation N2/M2 ≈ (7/2)e from first-order Doodson expansion
Back-solved eccentricity from N2/M2 = 0.19 under each approximation
quantitative_verification.computations: Python arithmetic: Doodson equilibrium coefficients M2=0.908, N2=0.174 give N2/M2 = 0.1916 (matches standard citation 0.1915). Using "N2/M2 ≈ 3e" with e=0.0549 gives 0.1647 — underpredicts observed 0.19 by 14%. Using "N2/M2 ≈ (7/2)e" gives 0.1922 — matches observed to <1%. Inverting: from N2/M2 = 0.19, the 3e formula back-solves to e = 0.0633 (15.4% above actual 0.0549); the (7/2)e formula back-solves to e = 0.0543 (1% below actual). This confirms holes_found #1. Period check: canonical N2 period is 12.6583 h (IHO / NOAA harmonic constituent tables); review states 12.6580 h — 3×10⁻⁴ h ≈ 1.08 s discrepancy. Confirms holes_found #4.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: Two numerical imprecisions in the review text itself: (a) detail_evidence uses the approximation "N2/M2 ≈ 3e" which is incorrect at leading order; the correct coefficient is 7/2. The back-solved eccentricity 0.063 is therefore 15% above the known lunar value 0.0549, and calling that "close" is an overclaim the correct formula easily fixes (1% agreement). (b) detail_evidence states the period as 12.6580 h; canonical value is 12.6583 h. Both are minor-to-moderate copyedits that sharpen the argument.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 3 assessment: WIN-051 has successfully absorbed the c2 major-rewrite recommendations. The detail_evidence now LEADS with the eccentricity argument (as c1 and c2 both recommended), the finding is differentiated from the other tidal WINs by citing eccentricity explicitly, and the detail_verdict_text includes the post-hoc/trivial-transcription structural frame from Part 3.1b. Verdict remains Self-Contradicted with high confidence (0.93).

The primary remaining issues are numerical, not structural:

1. The "3e" approximation is wrong (should be 7/2·e). This isn't catastrophic but it's embarrassing in a review that criticizes the dome for sloppy quantitative work. A one-sentence fix sharpens our strongest argument from "the dome's implied eccentricity is 15% off" to "the dome's implied eccentricity matches to 1%, but they didn't derive it."

2. The templated live-fetch language in detail_verdict_text misfires for N2 (which has no live fetch at all). The correct framing is strictly stronger — N2 has even less automation than M2/K1/S2 — but the current text obscures that by borrowing the wrong sentence.

3. The advocate-mode gap around non-uniform gear motion is the highest-impact rhetorical improvement. Without preempting it, a thoughtful defender can claim we're strawmanning by assuming Keplerian eccentricity is the only possible N2 origin. Two sentences close the gap.

Nothing here warrants a verdict change or a major rewrite — this is the "tighten the punch-line" phase. Confidence increases slightly from c2 (0.96) to c3 (0.93) not because the argument is weaker but because I've done the independent quantitative check and found the 15% numerical gap — I've discounted 3% for the remaining precision issues while noting they're easily patched.

One cross-cutting observation: the trivial-transcription language in detail_verdict_text ("It appears in undergraduate references and widely indexed resources, so there is no meaningful sense in which the dome monitor script 'predicted' it before reading the data") is powerful — this is the angle that survives any aetheric-defense move, because it cuts independent of the physical mechanism. Even if the dome ever DID derive N2 from non-uniform gear motion, the ORIGINAL timestamped claim would still be a transcription from Doodson 1921. Keeping that as a parallel track to the scientific critique is the right structure.

citations_verified: Doodson (1921) DOI 10.1098/rspa.1921.0088 resolves correctly to "The Harmonic Development of the Tide-Generating Potential," Proc. Royal Society A, Vol. 100, No. 704, pp. 305-329. The paper does derive the N2 constituent as part of the full harmonic expansion, and the equilibrium coefficients 0.90812 (M2) and 0.17387 (N2) are present in its Table II.
Lunar orbital eccentricity e = 0.0549 — JPL Horizons / standard astronomical constant. Verified.
N2/M2 amplitude ratio ≈ 0.19 — standard textbook value; computed here as 0.17387/0.90812 = 0.1915 from Doodson coefficients.
Monitor.py tidal domain coverage (M2, K1, S2 present; N2, O1 absent) — verified from raw-text/08-live-power.txt lines 212-245.
citations_failed: Internal review claim "N2/M2 ≈ 3e for small eccentricities yielding e ≈ 0.063 (close to the actual e ≈ 0.0549)" — the approximation is off by a factor of 7/6 at leading order and the back-solved e is 15% above the known value; "close" overclaims precision. See holes_found #1 and quantitative_verification.discrepancies.
Internal review claim "the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call" — for N2 there is no live fetch at all. See holes_found #3.
citations_unchecked: 18.613-year nodal cycle — well-established in celestial mechanics; not independently verified in this review.
Companion harmonics L2, 2N2, μ2 as co-predictable constituents — standard in harmonic analysis literature; not numerically verified here.
